content_of_post_to_answer: Why do we tend to treat our body like a transportation system for our brain?
answer: We often separate the brain from the body in our thinking, even though the brain is itself a biological organ dependent on the same system it seems to command. The “body as transport” metaphor hides a deeper mistake: it makes us ignore that movement, hunger, sleep, pain, and sensations are not inconveniences delivered to the mind but part of how the mind is created. The body is not a vehicle carrying consciousness; it is one of the conditions that makes consciousness possible.
